mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects accelerating manufacturing and utility output, which generates positive short-term market momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases consistently fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns, with a mean forward return below zero across all recovery phase samples.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with restrictive policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 70% of observations where both CPIAUCSL exceeds 195 and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market and expanding consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant predictive power for 60-day SPX direction, with a Rank IC consistently below 0.02 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirm a technical recession, triggering immediate portfolio de-risking and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 60% of confirmed recession onset periods, negating the recession risk premium.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and total employment signal synchronized economic acceleration, boosting near-term market sentiment and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 5-day horizons, with a Sharpe ratio below 0.5 in backtests.
